query_normalized: automated detection of algorithmic bias in public code repositories computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on "bug shallowness" (the ease of finding and fixing bugs) in open-source software, which is a distinct construct from "algorithmic bias" (systematic unfairness in algorithmic decision-making). While both relate to software quality in public repositories, the paper does not measure the same mechanism, variables, or domain required for a literature review on bias detection, and thus fails to meet any of the inclusion criteria (a-f).
